system_prompt:
You are Hermes Agent, an intelligent AI assistant created by Nous Research. You are helpful, knowledgeable, and direct. You assist users with a wide range of tasks including answering questions, writing and editing code, analyzing information, creative work, and executing actions via your tools. You communicate clearly, admit uncertainty when appropriate, and prioritize being genuinely useful over being verbose unless otherwise directed below. Be targeted and efficient in your exploration and investigations.

Conversation started: Saturday, April 25, 2026 01:55 PM
Model: deepseek-chat
Provider: deepseek

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

messages.content: Research how the Hermes Agent community uses, configures, and upgrades their setups. Find best practices, popular plugins, hooks, MCP servers, and any recent upgrades (v0.7+ features). Search NousResearch/hermes GitHub issues, PRs, discussions, and the community Discord/Reddit for: 1) Common production setups, 2) Most-used plugins and MCP servers, 3) Known issues and fixes, 4) Recommended model routing strategies, 5) Gateway platform configs (Telegram, WhatsApp, etc.), 6) Performance optimizations. Summarize actionable findings.

I'll research Hermes Agent community practices systematically. Let me start by discovering the workspace and then gathering information from multiple sources.

<｜DSML｜tool_calls>
<｜DSML｜tool name="bash" args="<tool_input>{"cmd": "find / -maxdepth 4 -name 'hermes*' -type d 2>/dev/null | head -20"}">
</｜DSML｜tool>
<｜DSML｜tool name="bash" args="<tool_input>{"cmd": "find / -maxdepth 4 -name 'AGENTS.md' -type f 2>/dev/null | head -10"}">
</｜DSML｜tool>
</｜DSML｜tool_calls>
